chief_complaint: A 17-year-old female presented with acute substernal chest pain radiating to the neck, accompanied by dyspnea and bilateral crepitus in the cervical region.
history: The patient developed spontaneous pneumomediastinum (SPM) following excessive shouting at a concert. She had no underlying pulmonary disease and no history of similar episodes. The patient was advised to refrain from strenuous vocalization and air travel.
exam: Physical examination revealed subcutaneous emphysema and a positive Hamman's sign. Vital signs were not provided.
labs: Chest radiography confirmed pneumomediastinum and subcutaneous emphysema, while computed tomography excluded underlying bullous disease.
